mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of boundary preprocessing in morphological image processing?

options.text: To extract and process image boundaries for clear definition
To apply color correction to boundary regions
To compress the entire image for storage
To remove all boundaries from the image

mcqs.explanation: Boundary preprocessing focuses specifically on extracting and processing image boundaries to produce a binary image where boundaries are clearly defined, which is essential for subsequent analysis.

mcqs.question: In boundary following, what does the algorithm do when it encounters a boundary point?

options.text: Marks the point as boundary and adds it to the chain
Immediately stops the tracing process
Changes the point's color to red
Skips to the next non-boundary point

mcqs.explanation: The boundary following algorithm marks each encountered boundary point and adds it to the chain, building a sequential record of the boundary as it traces the object's edge.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of using chain codes in boundary representation?

options.text: Compact and efficient storage of boundary information
Automatic color enhancement of boundaries
Real-time boundary smoothing during tracing
Conversion of boundaries to 3D models

mcqs.explanation: Chain codes provide a compact representation by encoding boundaries as sequences of directional codes, significantly reducing storage requirements compared to storing all boundary coordinates.

mcqs.question: Which statement best describes the relationship between boundary following and chain codes?

options.text: Boundary following generates the boundary points that chain codes then encode
Chain codes are applied before boundary following begins
They are completely independent techniques with no relationship
Chain codes replace the need for boundary following entirely

mcqs.explanation: Boundary following first traces and identifies the boundary points, and then chain codes are used to efficiently encode this sequence of points into directional instructions.

mcqs.question: What happens when the boundary following algorithm reaches the edge of the image?

options.text: The tracing stops as it has reached an image boundary
It wraps around to the opposite side of the image
It automatically reverses direction
It generates an error and terminates

mcqs.explanation: When the boundary following algorithm reaches the edge of the image, it stops tracing since it cannot continue beyond the image boundaries, completing the boundary extraction for that object.
